method_atom: a construction that embeds a single fixed point of a deformation problem into a moduli space of nearby solutions whose tangent space at that point is the space of admissible perturbations, equipped with a flat connection over the moduli space that transports and determines all correlators/observables consistently; combined with a homological quantization scheme (odd Laplacian / master equation on functions of a graded space) that, because pointwise products of admissible quantities fail to remain admissible, forces a global family-of-solutions viewpoint and yields a universal quantization of graded multi-operation algebraic structures decorated by the topology of one-higher-dimensional spaces.
object_atom: anomaly-free quantum field theories and their spaces of quantum observables, the associated d-algebra structures in d-dimensional QFT, and the algebro-differential-topology of (d+1)-manifolds and low-dimensional differential topology.
brief_reason: What carries across is the deformation-family-with-flat-connection plus BV-style homological quantization of graded algebras; what is domain-specific is that the deformed objects are QFTs, their observables, and manifold topology, and here method and object are tightly fused because the d-algebra machinery is defined directly on the QFT observable space.
